title: Error Handling in Real Projects
description: Apply error handling to real-world tasks: parse configuration files, validate required keys, safely convert types with descriptive errors, and process records with error collection.
starterCode:
# Exercise: Error Handling in Real Projects

def parse_config(config_str):
    """Parse key=value lines into a dict. Raise ValueError for malformed lines."""
    pass

def validate_config(config, required_keys):
    """Return a list of keys from required_keys that are missing in config."""
    pass

def safe_parse_int(value, field_name="value"):
    """Parse value as int. Raise ValueError with field_name in the message."""
    pass

def process_records(records, processor):
    """Process records collecting results and failures.
    Return dict with "processed" and "failed" lists."""
    pass
hints: Split config_str by newlines, then split each line by '=' with maxsplit=1
Strip whitespace from keys and values after splitting
Use a list comprehension to find missing keys in validate_config
Catch both ValueError and TypeError in safe_parse_int for robustness
Include field_name in the error message so callers know which field failed
process_records should continue after failures, collecting both results and errors

testCases.description: Test safe_parse_int with valid integer string
Test safe_parse_int includes field name in error
Test process_records with all successful records
Test parse_config raises ValueError for malformed lines
